reddit_title: Got hit by a commercial truck in Corpus Christi, TX — do I actually need a specialized lawyer or will any personal injury attorney do?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas — especially around heavy freight corridors like I-37 or the Port of Corpus Christi — are genuinely different from typical car accident claims. You're dealing with FMCSA federal regulations, potential liability spread across the driver, the trucking company, the cargo loader, and sometimes the truck manufacturer. An attorney who handles mostly fender-benders may not have experience subpoenaing electronic logging device data or fighting a carrier's rapid-response legal team.

The most important thing to know: evidence disappears fast. Trucking companies send their own investigators to accident scenes almost immediately. Black box data, driver logs, and maintenance records can be overwritten or lost if you don't have someone sending a legal preservation letter right away. Time is genuinely a factor here, not just a sales pitch.

If you're trying to find an attorney who specifically handles commercial truck cases in the Corpus Christi area, a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with lawyers from a network of 800+ firms who focus on exactly this type of case — without you having to cold-call offices and hope for the best. Whatever you do, don't just sign anything the trucking company's insurer sends you before talking to someone.
